name: Chicken Tagine with Apricots and Almonds. Serves 4. Active 30 min, total ~1.5 hrs
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Chicken Tagine with Apricots and Almonds. Serves 4. Active 30 min, total ~1.5 hrs.

Ingredients:
- 1 tsp ground cinnamon, 1 tsp ground ginger, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1 1/4 tsp salt
- 3 Tbsp + 1/4 cup olive oil
- 1 (3-lb) chicken, cut into 6 pieces (wings and backbone discarded)
- 1 Tbsp unsalted butter
- 1 medium red onion, halved and sliced 1/4" thick
- 4 garlic cloves, finely chopped
- 5 sprigs fresh coriander, 5 sprigs flat-leaf parsley
- 1 1/2 cups water
- 2 Tbsp mild honey
- 1 (3-inch) cinnamon stick
- 1/2 cup dried Turkish apricots, halved
- 1/3 cup whole blanched almonds
- 1 cup couscous (to serve)

Method:
1. Mix cinnamon, ginger, turmeric, pepper, 1 tsp salt, 2 Tbsp oil. Coat chicken.
2. Heat butter + 1 Tbsp oil in tagine (or skillet) over moderate heat. Brown chicken in 2 batches, skin-side down first, ~8–12 min total per batch. Transfer to plate.
3. Add onion + 1/4 tsp salt; cook ~8 min till soft. Add garlic, 3 min more.
4. Tie coriander + parsley into a bundle. Add to tagine with 1/2 cup water, chicken, and resting juices. Cover and simmer 30 min.
5. Meanwhile, simmer honey + 1 cup water + cinnamon stick + apricots until apricots are tender (top up water if needed). Reduce to a glaze, 10–15 min.
6. Heat remaining 1/4 cup oil in small skillet, fry almonds 1–2 min till golden, drain on paper towel.
7. Add apricot mix to tagine 10 min before chicken is done. Discard herb bundle and cinnamon stick. Serve with almonds sprinkled on top.

name: Beautiful courgette carbonara (Jamie Oliver)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Beautiful courgette carbonara (Jamie Oliver).

Shopping list (user's note): 3 courgettes, equal weight of mushrooms, 4 eggs, 100ml cream, bacon (12 slices), fresh thyme, Parmesan.

Ingredients (full):
- Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
- 6 medium green and yellow courgettes (user uses courgettes + mushrooms)
- 500g penne
- 4 large free-range or organic egg yolks
- 100 ml double cream
- 2 good handfuls freshly grated Parmesan
- Olive oil
- 12 thick slices pancetta or smoked streaky bacon, cut into chunky lardons
- Small bunch fresh thyme, leaves picked + chopped (reserve flowers if available)
- Optional: a few courgette flowers

Method:
1. Big pan of salted water to boil. Halve/quarter larger courgettes lengthwise, discard fluffy middle, slice on the angle to penne size. Smaller ones can just be sliced thinly.
2. Cook penne to packet instructions.
3. Carbonara sauce: in a bowl combine egg yolks, cream, half the Parmesan. Mix with fork. Season lightly. Set aside.
4. Big frying pan (35 cm), splash olive oil, fry pancetta/bacon till dark brown and crisp.
5. Add courgette slices + 2 big pinches black pepper. Sprinkle thyme leaves, stir to coat in bacon-flavoured oil. Fry till lightly golden and softened.
6. Drain pasta (reserve some cooking water). Toss pasta in pan with courgettes/bacon. OFF HEAT, add a ladle of cooking water + the egg sauce. Stir quickly so eggs don't scramble.
7. Sprinkle remaining Parmesan, more cooking water if needed for a silky shiny sauce. Taste for seasoning. Tear courgette flowers over if using. Serve immediately — sauce thickens fast.

name: Best Ever Scones (Edmonds). Prep 10 min, cook 15 min
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Best Ever Scones (Edmonds). Prep 10 min, cook 15 min.

Base:
- 3 cups Edmonds standard plain flour
- 6 tsp Edmonds baking powder
- 1/4 tsp salt
- 75g butter
- 1–1.5 cups milk (approx)
- Extra milk for glazing

Optional apple variation: apple, brown sugar, cinnamon, butter.

Method:
1. Oven 220°C. Sift flour, baking powder, salt into a bowl. Cut butter in until like fine breadcrumbs.
2. Add 1 cup milk; mix quickly with a knife to a soft dough, adding more milk if needed. Knead a few times.
3. Lightly dust an oven tray with flour. Press dough out on floured bench, cut into 12 even pieces. Arrange on tray, brush tops with milk, top half of oven.
4. Bake ~10 min till golden brown with crisp crusts. Cool on rack for crisp crust, or under a clean tea towel for soft scones.

Shortcut: Edmonds self-raising flour instead of plain + baking powder; or Edmonds Scone Mix (just add water).

name: Gordon Ramsay's Stuffed Chicken Roll Wrapped with Bacon with a Marsala Sauce. Makes 2 rolls (serves 4)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Gordon Ramsay's Stuffed Chicken Roll Wrapped with Bacon with a Marsala Sauce. Makes 2 rolls (serves 4).

Ingredients:
- 4 boned, skinned chicken thighs
- ~300g pork mince (user used minced softest pork medallions)
- 6 good rashers bacon
- 1/2 cup pistachios, chopped
- 4 sprigs thyme, chopped
- Handful parsley, chopped
- Sea salt, ground black pepper, cracked black pepper, olive oil

Method:
1. Combine pork mince, pistachios, herbs, salt + ground pepper. Splash olive oil if mince is lean. Check seasoning.
2. Lay 2 chicken thighs interlocking on a board. Place filling inside, roll up.
3. Lay 3 bacon rashers on board; place roll on top. Roll up tightly with bacon, trim excess.
4. Wrap tight in foil, fridge ≥2 hrs to set.
5. Repeat for second roll.
6. Drizzle with olive oil, crack black pepper over (no salt — bacon has it).
7. Fry until bacon is crispy (~20 min).
8. Rest on warm plate while making sauce.

Marsala sauce:
- 1.5 Tbsp sherry vinegar (or red wine vinegar)
- 2 Tbsp sweet Marsala wine (Boronia marsala is fine)
- 1/2 cup chicken stock
- 1 tsp sugar to taste

1. Deglaze pan with vinegar.
2. Add Marsala + stock, reduce to thick sauce.
3. Taste, add sugar to balance.

name: Prawn Thai red curry (Jamie Oliver 30-Minute Meals). Serves 4. Source: http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/food/article-1314862/
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Prawn Thai red curry (Jamie Oliver 30-Minute Meals). Serves 4. Source: http://www.dailymail.co.uk/femail/food/article-1314862/

Shopping list:
- 2 x 2cm fresh ginger
- 8 large unpeeled raw tiger prawns
- 200g sugar snap peas (or beans)
- 220g small cooked prawns
- 400g tin coconut milk
- Bag of prawn crackers
- 3 limes, 1 cucumber

Red curry paste ingredients (blitz):
- 2 stalks lemongrass (trimmed, bashed)
- 1 fresh red chilli
- 2 garlic cloves
- 4 kaffir lime leaves (optional)
- Bunch fresh coriander (stalks blended; leaves reserved)
- 2 jarred red peppers in oil
- 1 heaped tsp tomato puree
- 1 Tbsp fish sauce
- 2 Tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 2 cm fresh ginger, peeled

Method:
1. Oven 200°C/gas 6. Boil kettle.
2. Cucumber salad: grate 2 cm ginger onto a platter; add 1 Tbsp soy, 3 Tbsp evoo, 1 tsp sesame oil, juice of 1 lime. Peel cucumber into ribbons over platter, discard watery core. Sprinkle finely chopped coriander stalks (reserve leaves) and 1/2 finely chopped chilli. Don't toss till serving.
3. Jasmine rice: in a saucepan over medium, combine 1 mug basmati, salt, splash olive oil, 2 jasmine tea bags or 1 jasmine flower. Cover with 2 mugs boiling water. Cover, cook 7 min, off heat, lid on, steam 7 min.
4. Curry: blitz all paste ingredients to a paste. In a hot frying pan with olive oil, fry tiger prawns ~1 min. Add 1 Tbsp paste, fry 1 more min. Tip into an ovenproof dish, top shelf 8–10 min.
5. In the same pan over medium, drizzle olive oil, add sugar snaps then small prawns. Spoon in rest of paste; stir-fry 1–2 min. Add coconut milk, stir as it melts, simmer on medium-low.
6. Papaya platter: halve papayas, scoop seeds. Greek yoghurt in a small bowl with grated lime zest. Halve bananas lengthwise (skin on). Squeeze lime over platter, tear mint leaves over. Optional biscuits/macaroons.
7. Taste curry, correct with soy if needed, scatter coriander leaves. Serve with rice, prawn crackers, lime wedges, and the cucumber salad (now tossed).

name: Beef tagine (Jamie Oliver). Source: http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/beef-recipes/beef-tagine
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Beef tagine (Jamie Oliver). Source: http://www.jamieoliver.com/recipes/beef-recipes/beef-tagine

Ingredients:
- 600g stewing beef
- Olive oil
- 1 onion, peeled and finely chopped
- A small bunch of fresh coriander
- 1 x 400g tin chickpeas, drained
- 1 x 400g tin chopped tomatoes
- 800ml vegetable stock (organic preferred)
- 1 small squash (~800g), deseeded, cut into 5 cm chunks
- 100g prunes, stoned and roughly torn
- 2 Tbsp flaked almonds, toasted

Spice rub:
- Sea salt + black pepper
- 1 level Tbsp ras el hanout
- 1 level Tbsp ground cumin
- 1 level Tbsp ground cinnamon
- 1 level Tbsp ground ginger
- 1 level Tbsp sweet paprika

Method:
1. Mix spice rub. Massage into beef, cover, fridge 2 hrs (overnight ideal).
2. Heat generous olive oil in a tagine or casserole pan, fry beef 5 min over medium.
3. Add onion + coriander stalks; fry 5 min.
4. Add chickpeas, tomatoes, 400ml stock. Stir, bring to boil. Lid on (or foil), simmer 1.5 hrs.
5. Add squash, prunes, remaining stock. Stir, lid back on, cook 1.5 hrs more. Splash water if drying out.
6. Lid off; if too runny, simmer uncovered 5–10 min. Beef should be tender and flaking. Season.
7. Scatter coriander leaves and toasted almonds. Serve with lightly seasoned couscous.

Note: ras el hanout = "top of the shop" — Arabic blend of 10–100 spices, varies by vendor. Often nutmeg, cinnamon, mace, aniseed, turmeric, cayenne, peppercorns, dried galangal, ginger, cloves, cardamom, chilli, allspice, orris root.

name: Tray-baked cod with runner beans, pancetta and pine nuts (Jamie Oliver)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Tray-baked cod with runner beans, pancetta and pine nuts (Jamie Oliver).

Ingredients:
- 680g runner beans, trimmed and sliced on the angle
- 1 clove garlic, finely sliced
- Salt + freshly ground black pepper
- Olive oil
- 4 x 225g cod steaks, on the bone
- 12 slices smoked pancetta or streaky bacon
- 1 good handful pine nuts
- 2 lemons, halved

Method:
1. Oven 220°C / 425°F / gas 7. Put runner beans in a roasting tin with garlic, salt, pepper, just enough olive oil to coat. Mix.
2. Nestle cod steaks among the beans. Season again. Drape pancetta over cod and among beans. Sprinkle pine nuts. Drizzle olive oil. Add lemon halves to the tray (they go jammy and sweet).
3. Bake middle of oven ~15 min. Foil over the fish for the first 5 min, then remove. Cod is done when the centre bone lifts out easily. Serve with the tray juices drizzled over.

name: Dauphinoise potatoes (titled "dalphanoise pototoasss" in the original)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Dauphinoise potatoes (titled "dalphanoise pototoasss" in the original).

Ingredients:
- Knob of butter, for greasing
- 1 kg waxy potatoes (e.g. Desirée)
- 150 ml full-fat milk
- 142 ml carton double cream
- 1 garlic clove, peeled and halved
- 2 sprigs thyme
- Pinch freshly ground nutmeg
- 25 g parmesan, freshly grated

Method:
1. Oven 160°C / fan 140°C / gas 4. Line an 8 cm square brownie tin with greaseproof paper (no holes — liquid will seep), butter the paper.
2. Peel and slice potatoes to the width of a £1 coin. Pat dry.
3. Pour milk + cream into a pan, add garlic and thyme. Bring to boiling point, cool a little, strain into a jug. Sprinkle with nutmeg, keep warm. (Can be done ahead and reheated.)
4. Layer half the potatoes in the tin, overlapping, seasoning each layer with salt and pepper. Pour over half the liquid. Finish layering, add the rest of the liquid, scatter parmesan.
5. Bake 1–1.25 hrs until tender and golden on top.
6. Stand 5 min, cut into 6 portions, serve.

name: Pulled pork, the recipe i made
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Pulled pork, the recipe i made.

Source: http://www.simplyrecipes.com/recipes/pulled_pork_sandwich/

Get a pork shoulder. Bone in is better. ~1.5kg, about $15. Slice under skin and peel off — make crackling or discard.

BBQ pork rub (also good for ribs):
1/4 cup sugar, 1/4 cup brown sugar, 1/4 cup paprika, 3 Tbl coarse salt, 1 Tbl black pepper, 1 Tbl garlic powder, 1 Tbl onion powder, 1 Tbl chilli pepper, 1 Tbl cayenne. Makes ~5x what one shoulder needs — keeps in cupboard.

Rub all over pork, cover, fridge overnight.

Slow cooker: chopped medium/large onion + smashed garlic cloves, olive oil. Add sauce on top:
1/2 cup ketchup, 1/3 cup apple cider vinegar, 1/4 cup apple juice, 1/4 cup packed brown sugar, 1/4 cup tomato paste, 1/2 Tbsp paprika, 2 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce, 2 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper.

Add pork. Low for 10 hrs (recipe says); user prefers 14 hrs. Rotate pork after 5–6 hrs. Watch from 10 hrs — wait until dark/awesome.

Rest covered 20–45 min. Pour liquid into pot, add resting juices, separate fat (refrigerate till solid is easiest, then remove). Reduce to sauce, top up with apple cider vinegar / apple juice / ketchup to taste — user prefers vinegary. Sometimes adds rocket fuel.

Pull bone and excess fat. Shred with two forks; discard fat/sinew.

Stir half the bbq sauce into pulled pork to keep moist; reserve rest for serving. Reheats great. Freezes for lunches.

Serve: toast 3 ciabattas (New World), butter → pork → bbq sauce → coleslaw/lettuce → sliced gherkin → top bread.

name: Beef and bean burritos. Cook 30 min. Serves 4
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Beef and bean burritos. Cook 30 min. Serves 4.

Ingredients:
- 500g lean beef mince
- 300g can red kidney beans, drained, rinsed
- 1/2 cup coriander leaves, chopped
- 1/3 cup beef stock
- 400g can diced tomatoes
- 1 red capsicum, chopped
- 1 small brown onion, finely chopped
- 2 Tbsp tomato paste
- 1 Tbsp olive oil
- 1 garlic clove, crushed
- 1 tsp ground cumin
- 1 tsp smoked paprika
- 1/4 tsp chilli powder
- 8 flour tortillas
- 3/4 cup grated cheddar
- Sour cream + salad leaves, to serve

Method:
1. Oven 200°C. Heat oil in a large frying pan over medium-high. Add onion + garlic, cook 5 min till onion is tender. Add mince, cook 3 min till browned.
2. Add cumin, paprika, chilli powder; cook 1 min till aromatic. Add tomatoes, capsicum, tomato paste, stock. Bring to boil. Reduce to medium-low, simmer uncovered 10 min till thickened. Stir in beans and coriander.
3. Warm tortillas per packet. Divide beef along centre of each, roll up, place in a large greased baking dish. Sprinkle cheese on top.
4. Bake 10 min till cheese melts and burritos start to brown. Serve with sour cream and salad leaves.

name: Chinese chicken and corn soup (from Dumpling Sisters)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Chinese chicken and corn soup (from Dumpling Sisters).

SOUP: 640g tinned corn (4 small cans), 1 L chicken stock, 3/4 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp white pepper.
CHICKEN: 1 large chicken breast, 1/4 tsp light soy, 1/8 tsp salt.
SLURRY: 3 Tbsp tapioca flour or cornflour + 1/2 cup cold water.
EGGS: 3 eggs, 2 pinches salt.
TO SERVE: 2 sprigs spring onions, sesame oil.

Method:
1. Half the corn into a saucepan with a ladle of stock; blitz with stick blender to a thick, bitty paste (food processor or masher works too). This is "creamed corn" if you can't buy it (NZ supermarkets stock it).
2. Add remaining stock and whole corn kernels. Bring to boil.
3. Slice chicken thinly (a few mm thick, ~1 cm wide). Mix with salt + light soy.
4. Add chicken to boiling soup, loosen with chopsticks, return to boil.
5. Make slurry (cornflour stays thicker than tapioca). Off heat, slowly stir slurry in. Back on heat, bring to boil to cook the starch.
6. Whisk eggs with 2 pinches salt. On medium heat, slowly pour egg in while stirring continuously — pour faster for bigger ribbons.
7. Adjust salt + white pepper to taste. Stir in finely sliced spring onion.
8. Serve with extra spring onion and a few drops sesame oil.

name: Chicken breasts with crispy posh ham (Jamie Oliver style)
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Chicken breasts with crispy posh ham (Jamie Oliver style).

Ingredients:
- 2 sprigs fresh thyme
- 2 small skinless chicken breast fillets, ~120g each (higher welfare preferred)
- Freshly ground black pepper
- 1 lemon
- 4 slices prosciutto (recipe says 3 per breast = 6, but ingredient list says 4)
- Olive oil

Prep: pick thyme leaves. Score underside of breasts in a criss-cross with a small knife. Season with pepper only (prosciutto adds salt). Lay breasts side by side, sprinkle most of the thyme leaves over, grate a little lemon zest. Lay 3 slices of prosciutto on each breast, slightly overlapping. Drizzle olive oil, sprinkle remaining thyme. Cover with plastic wrap and bash with the bottom of a saucepan to ~1/2 inch thick.

Cook: medium heat in a frying pan. Remove plastic, transfer prosciutto-side down. Drizzle olive oil. 3 min each side, turning halfway, plus an extra 30 sec on the ham side to crisp.

Serve whole or sliced. Lemon wedges + olive oil drizzle. Good with mash + greens or a crunchy salad.

name: Lamb salad (Moroccan-spiced, with roast pumpkin and yoghurt). Serves 4
full_text:
Recipe from the user's Evernote archive — Lamb salad (Moroccan-spiced, with roast pumpkin and yoghurt). Serves 4.

LAMB MARINADE (rest ≥1 hr):
- 1 kg lamb backstrap, cut into 2 cm strips
- 1 Tbsp cumin, 1 Tbsp sweet paprika, salt
- 2 Tbsp chopped garlic
- 1 Tbsp chopped coriander, 1 Tbsp chopped continental parsley
- 1 Tbsp lemon juice
- Good drizzle olive oil

SALAD:
- 750g butternut pumpkin, deseeded, peeled, cut into 2 cm pieces
- Olive oil for drizzling
- 100g baby spinach leaves
- 2 Lebanese cucumbers, thinly sliced
- 55g (1/3 cup) pine nuts
- 2 tsp olive oil
- 2 Tbsp Moroccan seasoning
- 2 lamb eye of loin (backstraps), about 500g total — alternative to the marinated strips above
- 1 x 180g ctn South Cape Persian feta, drained, crumbled

YOGHURT SAUCE:
- 300g natural yoghurt
- 1 garlic clove, crushed
- 1/3 cup fresh mint leaves, chopped
- 1 Lebanese cucumber, finely chopped
- 1 small fresh red chilli, halved/deseeded/finely chopped
- 2 Tbsp water
- Salt + pepper

Method:
1. Oven 200°C. Roast pumpkin on tray with a little oil for 20 min till tender (or microwave covered, high, 4–5 min).
2. Combine yoghurt sauce ingredients in a bowl. Season.
3. In a frying pan over medium, toast pine nuts 2 min. Set aside.
4. Heat 2 tsp oil in pan over medium-high. Sprinkle Moroccan seasoning over lamb. Cook 4 min each side for medium. Rest 3 min covered with foil. Slice thickly across the grain.
5. Combine pumpkin, lamb, spinach, cucumber, pine nuts in a big bowl. Toss gently. Plate up. Top with feta. Serve with yoghurt sauce.

name: Oka fish (Pacific raw-fish dish). Note is a list of fish suitable for this dish: mahimahi, snapper, terakihi, wahu, tuna

name: General Cocktail Preparation Method: For all three - Add all ingredients to shaker with no ice and shake (dry shake). Add quite a bit of ice and shake again hard. I like them a little watered down hence lots of ice and hard shake. Strain into rocks glass through hawkthorn and sieve with one large block of ice. Cocktail notes: All citrus freshly squeezed, use about half an egg white per drink
full_text: General Cocktail Preparation Method: For all three - Add all ingredients to shaker with no ice and shake (dry shake). Add quite a bit of ice and shake again hard. I like them a little watered down hence lots of ice and hard shake. Strain into rocks glass through hawkthorn and sieve with one large block of ice. Cocktail notes: All citrus freshly squeezed, use about half an egg white per drink
